sentences.text: The following examples are provided as non-normative text to help explain the RISC-V atomics ISA.
insn:lr[]/insn:sc[] can be used to construct lock-free data structures.
sentences.section: Preamble > Atomics Assembly Code Examples > Compare-and-Swap Using LR/SC

sentences.text: An example using insn:lr[]/insn:sc[] to implement a compare-and-swap (CAS) function is shown in the following listing.
sentences.section: Preamble > Atomics Assembly Code Examples > Compare-and-Swap Using LR/SC

sentences.text: If inlined, CAS functionality only takes four instructions.
sentences.section: Preamble > Atomics Assembly Code Examples > Compare-and-Swap Using LR/SC

sentences.text: [source,asm] .Sample code for compare-and-swap function using LR/SC. -- a0 holds address of memory location a1 holds expected value a2 holds desired value a0 holds return value, 0 if successful, !0 otherwise cas: lr.w t0, (a0) Load original value. bne t0, a1, fail Doesn't match, so fail. sc.w t0, a2, (a0) Try to update. bnez t0, cas Retry if store-conditional failed. li a0, 0 Set return to success. jr ra Return. fail: li a0, 1 Set return to failure. jr ra Return. --
sentences.section: Preamble > Atomics Assembly Code Examples > Compare-and-Swap Using LR/SC

sentences.text: An example code sequence for a critical section guarded by a test-and-test-and-set (TTAS) spinlock is shown in the following listing.
sentences.section: Preamble > Atomics Assembly Code Examples > Compare-and-Swap Using LR/SC > Test-and-Test-and-Set Spinlock

sentences.text: Note that the first AMO is marked aq to order the lock acquisition before the critical section, and the second AMO is marked rl to order the critical section before the lock relinquishment.
sentences.section: Preamble > Atomics Assembly Code Examples > Compare-and-Swap Using LR/SC > Test-and-Test-and-Set Spinlock

sentences.text: We recommend the use of this AMO swap idiom for both lock acquire and release to simplify the implementation of speculative lock elision cite:[Rajwar:2001:SLE].
sentences.section: Preamble > Atomics Assembly Code Examples > Compare-and-Swap Using LR/SC > Test-and-Test-and-Set Spinlock

sentences.text: [source,asm] .Sample code for mutual exclusion. `a0` contains the address of the lock. -- li t0, 1 Initialize swap value. again: lw t1, (a0) Check if lock is held. bnez t1, again Retry if held. amoswap.w.aq t1, t0, (a0) Attempt to acquire lock. bnez t1, again Retry if held. ...
sentences.section: Preamble > Atomics Assembly Code Examples > Compare-and-Swap Using LR/SC > Test-and-Test-and-Set Spinlock

sentences.text: Critical section. ... amoswap.w.rl x0, x0, (a0) Release lock by storing 0. --
sentences.section: Preamble > Atomics Assembly Code Examples > Compare-and-Swap Using LR/SC > Test-and-Test-and-Set Spinlock

sentences.text: The following example code sequence illustrates the use of insn:amocas.d[] in a RV32 implementation to atomically increment a 64-bit counter.
sentences.section: Preamble > Atomics Assembly Code Examples > Compare-and-Swap Using LR/SC > Incrementing a 64-bit Counter Atomically on RV32

sentences.text: [source,asm] .Sample code for atomic 64-bit counter increment on 32-bit machine. -- a0 - address of the counter. increment: lw a2, (a0) Load current counter value using lw a3, 4(a0) two individual loads. retry: mv a6, a2 Save the low 32 bits of the current value. mv a7, a3 Save the high 32 bits of the current value. addi a4, a2, 1 Increment the low 32 bits. sltu a1, a4, a2 Determine if there is a carry out. add a5, a3, a1 Add the carry if any to high 32 bits. amocas.d.aqrl a2, a4, (a0) bne a2, a6, retry
sentences.section: Preamble > Atomics Assembly Code Examples > Compare-and-Swap Using LR/SC > Incrementing a 64-bit Counter Atomically on RV32

sentences.text: If amocas.d failed then retry bne a3, a7, retry using current values loaded by amocas.d. ret --
sentences.section: Preamble > Atomics Assembly Code Examples > Compare-and-Swap Using LR/SC > Incrementing a 64-bit Counter Atomically on RV32

sentences.text: The following example code sequence illustrates the use of insn:amocas.q[] to implement the enqueue operation for a non-blocking concurrent queue using the algorithm outlined in cite:[queue].
sentences.section: Preamble > Atomics Assembly Code Examples > Compare-and-Swap Using LR/SC > Non-blocking Concurrent Queue

sentences.text: The algorithm atomically operates on a pointer and its associated modification counter using the insn:amocas.q[] instruction to avoid the ABA problem.
sentences.section: Preamble > Atomics Assembly Code Examples > Compare-and-Swap Using LR/SC > Non-blocking Concurrent Queue

sentences.text: [source,asm] .Sample code for enqueue operation of a non-blocking concurrent queue. -- Data structures used by the queue: structure pointert {ptr: nodet *, count: uint64t} structure nodet {next: pointert, value: data type} structure queuet {Head: pointert, Tail: pointert} Inputs to the procedure: a0 - address of Tail variable a4 - address of a new node to insert at tail enqueue: ld a6, (a0) a6 = Tail.ptr ld a7, 8(a0) a7 = Tail.count ld a2, (a6) a2 = Tail.ptr->next.ptr ld a3, 8(a6) a3 = Tail.ptr->next.count ld t1, (a0) ld t2, 8(a0) bne a6, t1, enqueue Retry if Tail & next are not consistent bne a7, t2, enqueue Retry if Tail & next are not consistent bne a2, x0, movetail Was tail pointing to the last node? mv t1, a2 Save Tail.ptr->next.ptr mv t2, a3 Save Tail.ptr->next.count addi a5, a3, 1 Link the node at the end of the list amocas.q.aqrl a2, a4, (a6) bne a2, t1, enqueue Retry if CAS failed bne a3, t2, enqueue Retry if CAS failed addi a5, a7, 1 Update Tail to the inserted node amocas.q.aqrl a6, a4, (a0) ret Enqueue done movetail: Tail was not pointing to the last node addi a3, a7, 1 Try to swing Tail to the next node amocas.q.aqrl a6, a2, (a0) j enqueue Retry --
sentences.section: Preamble > Atomics Assembly Code Examples > Compare-and-Swap Using LR/SC > Non-blocking Concurrent Queue
